evals.prompt: Design a REST API for a task management app. Resources: users, projects, tasks, comments. Tasks belong to projects and have an assignee (user). Comments belong to tasks. Need CRUD for all resources, plus endpoints for assigning tasks and filtering tasks by status/priority. Use JWT auth.
evals.expected_output: Complete API spec with endpoints, HTTP methods, request/response schemas, auth, pagination, error format, and status codes

assertions.name: resource_oriented_urls
assertions.description: URLs use nouns (/projects, /tasks) not verbs (/getProjects)

assertions.name: crud_for_all
assertions.description: GET, POST, PUT/PATCH, DELETE for users, projects, tasks, comments

assertions.name: nested_resources
assertions.description: Tasks nested under projects (/projects/:id/tasks) or with projectId filter

assertions.name: pagination
assertions.description: List endpoints have pagination params (page/limit or cursor)

assertions.name: consistent_error_format
assertions.description: Error responses use consistent format with statusCode, error, message

assertions.name: auth_specified
assertions.description: JWT Bearer auth documented with protected vs public endpoints

evals.prompt: Design a REST API for a file storage service like S3. Endpoints: upload file (multipart), download file (presigned URL), list files with search/filter, delete file, create shared link with expiry. Files belong to folders, folders can be nested.
evals.expected_output: API spec for file operations with multipart upload, presigned URLs, recursive folder structure, and sharing links

assertions.name: multipart_upload
assertions.description: Upload endpoint accepts multipart/form-data

assertions.name: presigned_download
assertions.description: Download returns a presigned URL, not the file directly

assertions.name: folder_nesting
assertions.description: API supports nested folder structure

assertions.name: shared_links
assertions.description: Endpoint to create time-limited shared links

assertions.name: search_filter
assertions.description: List endpoint supports search by name and filter by type/size

evals.prompt: Design a GraphQL API for a social media feed. Types: User (profile), Post (content, images, likes), Comment (on posts), Follow (user follows user). Queries: feed (paginated, sorted by relevance), user profile, post detail. Mutations: create post, like/unlike, follow/unfollow, add comment.
evals.expected_output: GraphQL schema with types, queries, mutations, input types, pagination (cursor-based), and auth directives

assertions.name: graphql_types
assertions.description: Type definitions for User, Post, Comment, Follow

assertions.name: queries_defined
assertions.description: Query type with feed, user, post queries

assertions.name: mutations_defined
assertions.description: Mutation type with createPost, like, follow, addComment

assertions.name: cursor_pagination
assertions.description: Feed uses cursor-based pagination (first/after pattern)

assertions.name: input_types
assertions.description: Input types for mutations (CreatePostInput, etc.)

evals.prompt: Design API versioning strategy for our public REST API. We currently have v1 with 20 endpoints. We need to make breaking changes to 5 endpoints (rename fields, change response shapes) without breaking existing clients. What approach should we use?
evals.expected_output: Versioning strategy (URL path, header, or query param) with migration plan, deprecation timeline, and backward compatibility guidance

assertions.name: versioning_approach
assertions.description: Recommends a specific versioning approach with rationale

assertions.name: migration_plan
assertions.description: Plan for transitioning clients from v1 to v2

assertions.name: deprecation_timeline
assertions.description: Timeline for v1 deprecation with sunset headers

assertions.name: backward_compatibility
assertions.description: How to maintain v1 while serving v2

assertions.name: documentation_strategy
assertions.description: How to document both versions

evals.prompt: Design a webhook API for our payment platform. Merchants register webhook URLs, subscribe to event types (payment.completed, payment.failed, refund.created, dispute.opened), and receive POST callbacks. Need signature verification, retry logic, and a delivery log endpoint.
evals.expected_output: Webhook API spec with registration, event types, HMAC signature, retry policy, delivery status endpoint

assertions.name: registration_endpoint
assertions.description: Endpoint to register/update/delete webhook URLs with event type subscriptions

assertions.name: event_types
assertions.description: Documented event types with payload schemas for each

assertions.name: signature_verification
assertions.description: HMAC-SHA256 signature in header for payload verification

assertions.name: retry_policy
assertions.description: Retry schedule documented (exponential backoff, max attempts)

assertions.name: delivery_logs
assertions.description: Endpoint to query delivery history with status and response codes
